title: Drug-Target Binding Affinity Classification (PWDR)
sub_domain: Quantum-chemistry binding-energy estimation with affinity-class categorical readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 physical_with_discrete_readout (PWDR) genesis Principle. Wraps L1-294 DFT (existing in v1 genesis) with deterministic binding-affinity classification via free-energy thresholds. Universal in computational drug discovery. UTSW Drug Discovery Institute and Simmons Cancer Center oncology drug development.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on binding-affinity categorical readout {non-binder, weak, moderate, strong, ultra-strong}

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Affinity class(drug, target) = step function on Delta-G_binding [kcal/mol] (or equivalently log10(K_d)): non-binder if Delta-G > -5 (K_d > 10 micromolar); weak if -7 < Delta-G < -5 (K_d in 10 nanomolar - 10 micromolar); moderate if -9 < Delta-G < -7 (K_d in 100 picomolar - 10 nanomolar); strong if -11 < Delta-G < -9 (K_d in 1 picomolar - 100 picomolar); ultra-strong if Delta-G < -11. Drug-likeness category overlay (Lipinski Rule of 5 + Veber rules) applies independently. Delta-G_binding recovered via FEP (free-energy perturbation), TI (thermodynamic integration), MM-PBSA, or DFT-corrected docking.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: binding free energy Delta-G_binding [kcal/mol] of drug-target complex recovered from DFT-corrected molecular dynamics or FEP simulation
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Affinity classification is a step function on continuous Delta-G_binding values; per-class boundaries form measure-zero hyperplanes at Delta-G = {-5, -7, -9, -11} kcal/mol. Smoothed-classification globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.10 per kcal/mol of energy uncertainty.

related_principles: L1-294 (DFT — quantum-chemistry energy minimization core, physics_core_principle)
L1-298 (ab initio MD — sibling for dynamic free-energy calculations, informational)
L1-412 (protein folding energy landscape — sibling computational biology, n_c=4, informational)

E.description: Drug-Target Binding Affinity Classification (PWDR): wraps L1-294 DFT analytical core with established medicinal-chemistry affinity-class thresholds. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-294): compute drug-target binding free energy Delta-G_binding via FEP / TI / MM-PBSA / DFT-corrected docking using DFT-derived atomic charges and force-field parameters. Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply standard medicinal-chemistry thresholds at Delta-G = {-5, -7, -9, -11} kcal/mol to assign categorical label. Difficulty tier delta = 5. Mismatch parameters: force_field_uncertainty, sampling_convergence_error, water_model_uncertainty, protein_flexibility_truncation, drug_protonation_state, conformational_search_incompleteness.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-294 + MD/FEP): drug + target -> equilibrium ensemble -> Delta-G_binding via thermodynamic cycle. Stage 2: affinity_class = step(Delta-G_binding at [-11, -9, -7, -5] kcal/mol).
E.world_state_x: Per-(drug, target) pair categorical affinity class in {non_binder, weak, moderate, strong, ultra_strong}.
E.observation_y: Drug SMILES / 3D structure + target protein PDB structure (or homology model); FEP simulation trajectory or docking pose ensemble.

G.dag: L.molecular_topology -> L.dft_charge_calculation -> L.force_field_parameterization -> L.molecular_dynamics -> L.fep_thermodynamic_cycle -> L.binding_free_energy -> L.affinity_threshold_classifier -> int.ensemble
coupling_constraints: dft_to_force_field: DFT-derived atomic charges and parameters couple to force-field MD; alchemical FEP cycle couples bound and unbound states through thermodynamic cycle

W.regime: Existence guaranteed within Omega bounds. Uniqueness conditional on adequate sampling (typically ≥100 ns of FEP for druglike ligands) and convergent thermodynamic cycle. Stability dominated by force_field_uncertainty (~1-2 kcal/mol systematic error) and sampling_convergence_error. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled DFT + MD + FEP + threshold forward established by Wang 2015 (FEP+ benchmark), Mey 2020 (best practices for FEP), Cournia 2020 (relative binding free energy review), Lipinski 1997 (Rule of 5), Veber 2002 (drug-likeness rules), Hopkins 2004 (ligand efficiency).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + statistical sampling [FEP / TI / replica-exchange MD] + categorical-readout [affinity threshold] | machine-learned [deep neural force fields, Schrodinger FEP+] | linear-operator + deep neural [DeepBind, AtomNet]
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-294 DFT bound on charge accuracy; FEP sampling bound: |Delta-G_hat - Delta-G_true| <= C1 * sigma_force_field + C2 / sqrt(simulation_time). Misclassification probability bound: Pr(class_hat != class_true) <= C_thresh * |Delta-G_error| / d(Delta-G, tau_class_boundary).
C.complexity: O((N_atoms_drug + N_atoms_target)^2 * simulation_time_ns * N_lambda_windows) for FEP; expensive for large complexes

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Predict drug-target binding affinity / classify binders' (could be data_driven_statistical via deep neural networks like DeepBind / AtomNet). Option-3 recast: 'Compute Delta-G_binding via DFT + FEP / TI / MM-PBSA quantum-chemistry; apply medicinal-chemistry affinity-class thresholds at -5, -7, -9, -11 kcal/mol'. Feasible because binding free energy has a well-defined quantum-statistical-mechanics forward model and affinity classes are deterministic functions of recovered Delta-G.

joint_well_posedness_references: Wang L et al. (2015) Accurate and reliable prediction of relative ligand binding potency in prospective drug discovery by way of a modern FEP protocol. J Am Chem Soc 137(7):2695-2703.
Mey ASJS et al. (2020) Best practices for alchemical free energy calculations. Living J Comput Mol Sci 2(1):18378.
Cournia Z, Allen B, Sherman W (2020) Relative binding free energy calculations in drug discovery: recent advances and practical considerations. J Chem Inf Model 57(12):2911-2937.

Lipinski CA, Lombardo F, Dominy BW, Feeney PJ (1997) Experimental and computational approaches to estimate solubility and permeability in drug discovery and development settings. Adv Drug Deliv Rev 23(1-3):3-25.
Veber DF, Johnson SR, Cheng HY, Smith BR, Ward KW, Kopple KD (2002) Molecular properties that influence the oral bioavailability of drug candidates. J Med Chem 45(12):2615-2623.
Hopkins AL, Groom CR, Alex A (2004) Ligand efficiency: a useful metric for lead selection. Drug Discov Today 9(10):430-431.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-294 DFT recovers electronic structure for any molecule / lattice. L1-521 specializes to drug-target complex binding free energy + adds affinity-class threshold readout. Different solution_space (1D affinity class vs continuous electronic-structure energy); adds L.fep_thermodynamic_cycle, L.binding_free_energy, L.affinity_threshold_classifier primitives.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Drug-target binding affinity classification is the canonical workflow in lead-optimization / hit-to-lead drug discovery. FDA-relevant for IND-enabling preclinical packages. Commercial systems: Schrodinger FEP+, OpenEye OEDocking, Cresset Spark. UTSW Drug Discovery Institute uses computational binding-affinity tools for tumor-target programs. Active research at Simmons Cancer Center oncology drug development. Reference solvers: GROMACS / AMBER / NAMD (MD); Schrodinger / OpenMM (FEP); MM-PBSA scripts. PWDR formulation gives physics-grounded explainability: affinity class is derived from explicit Delta-G_binding tied to medicinal-chemistry conventions, not black-box prediction.
